key_insight: The paper extends FFT-based mechanical solvers to handle both periodic and non-periodic boundary conditions in parallel, using discrete trigonometric transforms and Green-operator preconditioning for robust linear and nonlinear analysis.
engineering_relevance: This enables efficient large-scale simulation of realistic structures and microstructures under mixed loading and boundary conditions, including elasticity, plasticity, and crystal plasticity.
method: Uses a displacement-based fixed-point FFT scheme with convergence acceleration
Adapts the classical Moulinec-Suquet method via discrete trigonometric transforms for Dirichlet/Neumann boundaries
Builds discrete Green operators with finite-difference preconditioners and a double tetrahedron scheme for non-periodic problems
evidence_signal: The solver is evaluated on non-trivial load cases, densely discretized microstructures, and multiple material laws in both small- and finite-strain settings, with comparisons to a classical hexahedral scheme.
watch_item: Performance and accuracy depend on the chosen reference material/preconditioner and on the boundary-condition-specific transform/discretization setup, which may affect implementation complexity.
paper_title: A robust and versatile parallel FFT-based mechanical solver for general non-periodic and periodic boundary conditions
